Title: miR1432-OsACOT (Acyl-CoA thioesterase) module determines grain yield via enhancing grain filling rate in rice

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Fatty acid desaturation and elongation, especially C16:0 to C18:2-related branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies/modulates fatty acid pool and composition that supports grain filling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes acyl-CoA thioesters into free fatty acids and coenzyme A
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Medium-chain and major grain fatty acids, especially palmitic acid (16:0), stearic acid (18:0), oleic acid (18:1), and linoleic acid (18:2)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports lipid metabolism, membrane/endomembrane organization, auxin transport-associated processes, and grain filling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes grain filling rate, increases grain size, 1000-hulled grain weight, and grain yield; shifts fatty acid composition toward higher 18:1 and 18:2 and lower 16:0 and 18:0
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: miR1432-resistant overexpression in rice endosperm (OXmACOT); qRT-PCR; transient co-expression assay in Nicotiana benthamiana; RLM-5' RACE cleavage mapping; GC-MS fatty acid profiling; field trials
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly expressed in roots and developing seeds; analyzed in endosperm at 10 DAF
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly expressed in roots and developing seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown, non-stressed conditions; developing endosperm/grain filling stage
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of WT, STTM1432 and OXmiR1432 endosperm at 10 DAF; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point in grain filling-associated fatty acid metabolism, but not definitively established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout attempts failed to regenerate seedlings, suggesting essential function or lethality under deficiency; identified as a major direct target of miR1432
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Endosperm-specific expression of miR1432-resistant OsACOT is a strong strategy to improve grain weight and yield in rice
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: OsACOT deficiency appears lethal; no major penalty in measured panicle traits for overexpression lines reported
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsACOT (LOC_Os04g35590) was identified as the authentic direct target of miR1432 by transient assay and RLM-RACE. 2. miR1432-resistant OsACOT overexpression phenocopied miR1432 suppression, increasing grain filling rate, grain size, 1000-grain weight, and yield. 3. OsACOT altered grain fatty acid composition, decreasing 16:0/18:0 and increasing 18:1/18:2, linking fatty acid metabolism to improved grain filling.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively regulates OsACOT expression through cleavage
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Fatty acid metabolism and associated hormone homeostasis during grain filling
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Fatty acids and grain-filling-associated hormone-linked storage accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing grain/endosperm; brown rice for fatty acid measurement
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports determination of grain yield via effects on lipid metabolism and IAA/ABA-associated grain filling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator of grain filling, grain size, 1000-hulled grain weight, and yield
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Endosperm-specific overexpression and STTM suppression in rice; northern blot; stem-loop qRT-PCR; target validation by transient assay and RLM-RACE; grain filling measurements; field trials; RNA-seq; hormone quantification
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in root, seedling, stem, and developing seeds; expression increases during seed development and is negatively correlated with grain filling rate
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in root, seedling, stem, and developing seeds
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown, non-stressed conditions; developing endosperm/grain filling stage
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-specific negative regulator of grain filling and yield through direct repression of OsACOT
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suppression of miR1432 using STTM is a promising engineering strategy to increase grain weight and yield
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression reduces grain size and yield substantially; suppression showed no notable adverse effects on measured panicle traits
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: STTM1432 increased plot yield up to 17.14% and per-plant yield by ~14.90-17.61% in field trials
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of WT, STTM1432 and OXmiR1432 endosperm at 10 DAF; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. miR1432 is highly expressed in developing seeds and increases during grain development, inversely correlating with grain filling rate. 2. Endosperm-specific suppression of miR1432 increases grain filling rate, grain size, and yield, whereas overexpression causes the opposite effects. 3. miR1432 directly cleaves OsACOT and thereby modulates fatty acid metabolism and IAA/ABA homeostasis associated with grain filling.
